title: Dynamic Rendering Decision and Isolated Browser-Service Boundary · Failure Repair Bay
summary: A preserved Go crawl trace contains one plausible scope, URL, robots, transport, body, tokenizer, goquery, frontier, goroutine, Colly callback, report, security, or shutdown defect; identify the first broken invariant before editing. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, a…
objectives: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.
Define typed request, origin policy, viewport, actions, readiness, budgets, response, errors, trace, and cancellation for a separate controlled service.
Apply authorization, SSRF, resource type, download, WebSocket, worker, and byte rules to every browser-initiated request.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity
steps.instruction:
debug case — A debug Go team handles case 5281 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to prove render necessity, typed browser port, subresource policy, untrusted results, and lifecycle evidence; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one fuzz, race, mutation, capacity, binary, deployment, incident, restore, rollback, or residual-risk condition and reconcile release evidence. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering.

Inspect the Render Necessity trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for require evidence that static retrieval cannot meet an authorized stakeholder need before admitting browser rendering. For Authorized site auditing and Go Inspect the failure record · Debug Render Necessity, keep Render Necessity connected to executable Go authorization, URL, robots, response, tree, frontier, goroutine, report, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

options.text: The record names the assumption, applies Render Necessity, and reports a changed-case check tied to the stakeholder decision.

The Render Necessity record says least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract looks right but supplies no governing condition, observation, changed case, or counterexample. That claim fails the inspect the failure record evidence boundary for Render Necessity.

For inspect the failure record, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof. This does not satisfy Render Necessity for least-authority dynamic-rendering service contract.
